description:
## Goal
Define the unified intermediate representation (IR) types that all of Abstractfeld flows through. This is the single AST shared by Julia, Lean, LaTeX, and every future backend.

## Deliverables
- `src/ir/types.jl` — Julia structs:
  ```julia
  abstract type Expr end
  struct Lit <: Expr; val::Rational{BigInt}; end
  struct Sym <: Expr; name::Symbol; end
  struct Idx <: Expr; name::Symbol; pos::IndexPos; end  # pos ∈ {Up, Down}
  struct App <: Expr; op::Symbol; args::Vector{Expr}; end
  struct Bind <: Expr; binder::Symbol; var::Expr; body::Expr; metadata::Vector{Expr}; end
  struct Ann <: Expr; expr::Expr; ann::Annotation; end
  ```
- `@enum IndexPos Up Down`
- `Annotation` type (initially: `SymmetryAnn`, `TypeAnn`)
- Convenience constructors: `lit(1//2)`, `sym(:x)`, `idx(:a, Down)`, `app(:+, e1, e2)`, `tensor(:R, idx(:a,Down), idx(:b,Down), ...)`
- `Base.:(==)` and `Base.hash` for all IR types (structural equality)
- Export all public types and constructors from `Abstractfeld` module

## Design constraints
- Immutable structs (no mutation after construction)
- `Rational{BigInt}` for exact arithmetic (no floats in IR)
- `Symbol` for names (interned strings, fast comparison)
- `Vector{Expr}` for args (not tuple — variable arity)

## Acceptance
- All 6 IR node types construct correctly
- Equality and hashing work: `app(:+, lit(1), lit(2)) == app(:+, lit(1), lit(2))`
- Can represent: `R_{abcd}`, `∫₀^∞ e^{-px} dx`, `x + y * z`
- Unit tests pass
title: Core IR types

description:
## Goal
Bootstrap the Julia package structure for Abstractfeld.jl.

## Deliverables
- `Project.toml` with metadata (name, UUID, version 0.1.0-dev)
- Dependencies: Metatheory.jl (≥3.0), TermInterface.jl, DuckDB.jl, JSON3.jl, HTTP.jl, SHA (stdlib)
- `src/Abstractfeld.jl` — top-level module with submodule includes
- `test/runtests.jl` — test harness with Test stdlib
- Directory skeleton: `src/ir/`, `src/egraph/`, `src/search/`, `src/kb/`, `src/bridge/`
- `.gitignore` for Julia artifacts (Manifest.toml, .julia/, etc.)

## Acceptance
- `julia --project=. -e 'using Abstractfeld'` loads without error
- `julia --project=. -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.test()'` runs (empty) test suite
title: Julia package scaffold

description:
## Goal
Design and implement the minimal KB schema for storing verified mathematical results.

## Deliverables
- `src/kb/schema.jl` — DuckDB table definitions:
  - `results` table: id, expr_hash (BLOB), expr_sexpr (TEXT), result_sexpr (TEXT), verification_level (INT 0-4), lean_proof_artifact (TEXT), created_at, provenance (TEXT)
  - `fingerprints` table: result_id, point_hash (BLOB), numerical_value (DOUBLE) — for L2 fuzzy matching
- `src/kb/init.jl` — create/open database, run migrations
- Schema supports the verification lattice: L0 (unverified) through L4 (formally verified)
- Provenance is append-only text log per result

## Design constraints
- Single-file DuckDB database (distributable)
- Structural hash is SHA-256 of canonical S-expression (from A5)
- No ORM — raw SQL via DuckDB.jl for simplicity

## Acceptance
- Can create DB, insert a row, query by expr_hash
- Schema matches verification lattice levels L0–L4
title: Minimal knowledge base schema

description:
## Goal
Bootstrap the Lean 4 project for Abstractfeld's verification kernel.

## Deliverables
- `lean/lakefile.lean` — Lake project config with Mathlib dependency
- `lean/lean-toolchain` — pinned to Mathlib-compatible Lean 4 version
- `lean/Abstractfeld.lean` — root import file
- Directory skeleton: `lean/Abstractfeld/IR/`, `lean/Abstractfeld/Tensor/`, `lean/Abstractfeld/Bridge/`
- Verify Mathlib imports resolve: `import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Multilinear.Basic`, `import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.ExteriorAlgebra.Basic`

## Acceptance
- `cd lean && lake build` succeeds with Mathlib resolved
- A trivial `#check AlternatingMap` compiles, confirming Mathlib availability
title: Lean4 project scaffold

description:
## Goal
Define the Lean 4 inductive type that mirrors the Julia IR exactly. This is the Lean-side representation that proofs operate on.

## Deliverables
- `lean/Abstractfeld/IR/Expr.lean`:
  ```lean
  inductive IndexPos | up | down
  
  inductive Expr
    | lit (val : ℚ)
    | sym (name : String)
    | idx (name : String) (pos : IndexPos)
    | app (op : String) (args : List Expr)
    | bind (binder : String) (var : Expr) (body : Expr) (metadata : List Expr)
    | ann (expr : Expr) (ann : Annotation)
  ```
- `lean/Abstractfeld/IR/Annotation.lean` — annotation inductive (symmetry, type)
- `deriving DecidableEq, Repr, Hashable` on `Expr`
- Basic operations: `Expr.size`, `Expr.depth`, `Expr.freeVars`

## Design constraints
- Must be structurally identical to Julia IR (same constructors, same field names conceptually)
- Use `ℚ` from Mathlib for rationals (not `Float`)
- Use `String` for names (Lean doesn't have Symbol; conversion happens at serialization boundary)
- `List Expr` for args (mirrors Julia's Vector{Expr})

## Acceptance
- `lake build` succeeds
- Can construct `R_{abcd}`: `Expr.app "tensor" [Expr.sym "R", Expr.idx "a" .down, ...]`
- `DecidableEq` works: two structurally identical exprs compare equal
title: Lean4 IR mirror inductive type
